files..mockery.yaml:
with-expecter: true
all: false
packages:
  fec_pool_service/internal/usecase:
    interfaces:
      IFecPoolServiceUseCase:
        config:
          dir: internal/usecase/mocks
          outpkg: mocks
          filename: IFecPoolServiceUseCase.go
  fec_pool_service/internal/repository:
    interfaces:
      IFecPoolServiceRepository:
        config:
          dir: internal/repository/mocks
          outpkg: mocks
          filename: IFecPoolServiceRepository.go
files.BUGFIX_NOTES.md:
# Bugfix Notes

## #1 — SQL Injection Risk
**Files:** `internal/util/query_validator.go` (new), `internal/usecase/pool_service_usecase.go`, `internal/usecase/usecase.go`, `config/config.go`

Service allowed clients to send arbitrary SQL directly to Oracle DB. Added `ValidateQuery()` with three layers of protection:
- Prefix whitelist (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE, BEGIN, CALL, DECLARE)
- Blocked keywords (DROP, TRUNCATE, ALTER, CREATE, GRANT, REVOKE, PURGE) — checked against string-literal-stripped query
- Multi-statement detection: simple statements allow only a trailing `;`, block statements (BEGIN/CALL/DECLARE) allow `;` inside but reject content after the final `;`

All rules are configurable via `query_security` in ETCD/env without redeployment.

## #2 — Panic Swallowed Silently in ExecuteProcedure
**File:** `internal/repository/pool_service_repo.go`

`recover()` caught panics but returned `(nil, nil, nil)` — caller treated it as success. Fixed by using named return values so `recover()` assigns `err = fmt.Errorf("panic recovered...")`, allowing the caller to detect the failure.

## #3 Defer in Loop + double Close RefCursor
**File:** `internal/repository/pool_service_repo.go`

`defer` inside a `for` loop caused all closures to capture the same `refCursor` variable (last iteration value) — earlier cursors leaked, last cursor was closed multiple times. Fixed by extracting cursor processing into a dedicated `processCursor()` method — each cursor gets its own scope with a proper `defer Close`. Also fixed a nil pointer dereference when logging `refCursor.MaxRowSize` on a nil cursor.

## #4 — Index Out of Bounds in Auth
**File:** `internal/api/pool_service_apis.go`

`AuthFuncOverride` accessed `authorizationArrays[1]` without length check. A header value of `"Basic"` (no base64 payload) or an empty string caused a panic. Fixed by adding guards: `len(authorization) == 0`, `authorization[0] == ""`, `len(authorizationArrays) != 2`.

## #5 — DML Retry on Same Broken Connection
**Files:** `internal/repository/pool_service_repo.go`, `config/config.go`

Retried 3 times on the same dead connection — all attempts failed. Also retried on all error types, risking duplicate writes. Fixed:
- Only retry on `ErrBadConn` (connection-level errors); DB logic errors (constraint violations, syntax errors) fail immediately
- On bad connection: close the broken one, acquire a new connection from the pool
- Moved `maxRetries` from hardcoded constant to config (`oracledb.max_retries`)

## #6 — Double time.Duration Conversion
**File:** `cmd/main.go`

`time.Duration(time.Duration(x))` double-wrapped in 5 places across gRPC keepalive config. Simplified to `time.Duration(x) * time.Unit`.

## #7 — Timeout Returns nil gRPC Error
**File:** `internal/api/pool_service_apis.go`

ExecuteQuery timeout returned `nil` as gRPC error — client received HTTP 200 with error details buried in the response body. Fixed by returning `status.Errorf(codes.DeadlineExceeded, ...)` so gRPC gateway maps to HTTP 504 and client-side retry interceptors can detect the failure properly.

## #8 — Goroutine Leak + Worker Pool Slot Mismatch
**File:** `internal/api/pool_service_apis.go`

Worker pool slot was released when the handler returned (via `defer` in handler scope), but the goroutine still held the DB connection. Fixed by moving `<-a.workerPool` into the goroutine's `defer` — slot is only released when the query actually completes, ensuring slot count always matches actual connection usage.

## #9 — reflect.ValueOf(nil).Elem() Panic
**File:** `internal/usecase/pool_service_usecase.go`

When an Oracle procedure returned NULL for an OUT parameter, `reflect.ValueOf(nil).Elem()` panicked. Fixed by adding nil check, pointer-nil check (`rv.Kind() == reflect.Ptr && rv.IsNil()`), and a non-pointer fallback path.
files.config/config.yaml:
# fec_pool_service configuration
# Place this file at /etc/config/config.yaml or set CONFIGMAP__PATH env var
# All values can be overridden by env vars using double underscore as separator
# Example: ORACLEDB__HOST=10.0.0.1 overrides oracledb.host

environment: production

oracledb:
  host: "10.30.121.7"
  port: 1521
  username: "app_esb_microsvc"
  secure_password: "5LNmaonbq7oUkshx0vOThb3LqjuwwEeCWn/KmVD8Xa/AxA1D6x6I2AWFnESz7SrBFrpTRa+p8rX6TA=="
  service_name: "cfappuat"
  max_connection: 20
  min_connection: 5
  connection_life_time: 30
  max_retries: 3

routine_nbr: 20

query_security:
  allowed_prefixes:
    - SELECT
    - INSERT
    - UPDATE
    - DELETE
    - MERGE
    - BEGIN
    - CALL
    - DECLARE
    - WITH
  blocked_keywords:
    - DROP
    - TRUNCATE
    - ALTER
    - CREATE
    - GRANT
    - REVOKE
    - PURGE
  allow_multi_statement: false
files.docs/README.md:
# third_party/OpenAPI

This directory contains HTML, Javascript, and CSS assets that dynamically generate Swagger documentation from a Swagger-compliant API definition in [example.swagger.json](./example.swagger.json) file. That file is auto-generated by running `make generate` in the root of this repository. The static assets are copied from [this dist folder](https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/tree/master/dist) of the OpenAPI-UI project.
files.docs/oauth2-redirect.html:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en-US">
<body onload="run()">
</body>
</html>
<script>
    'use strict';
    function run () {
        var oauth2 = window.opener.swaggerUIRedirectOauth2;
        var sentState = oauth2.state;
        var redirectUrl = oauth2.redirectUrl;
        var isValid, qp, arr;

        if (/code|token|error/.test(window.location.hash)) {
            qp = window.location.hash.substring(1);
        } else {
            qp = location.search.substring(1);
        }

        arr = qp.split("&")
        arr.forEach(function (v,i,_arr) { _arr[i] = '"' + v.replace('=', '":"') + '"';})
        qp = qp ? JSON.parse('{' + arr.join() + '}',
                function (key, value) {
                    return key === "" ? value : decodeURIComponent(value)
                }
        ) : {}

        isValid = qp.state === sentState

        if ((
          oauth2.auth.schema.get("flow") === "accessCode"||
          oauth2.auth.schema.get("flow") === "authorizationCode"
        ) && !oauth2.auth.code) {
            if (!isValid) {
                oauth2.errCb({
                    authId: oauth2.auth.name,
                    source: "auth",
                    level: "warning",
                    message: "Authorization may be unsafe, passed state was changed in server Passed state wasn't returned from auth server"
                });
            }

            if (qp.code) {
                delete oauth2.state;
                oauth2.auth.code = qp.code;
                oauth2.callback({auth: oauth2.auth, redirectUrl: redirectUrl});
            } else {
                let oauthErrorMsg
                if (qp.error) {
                    oauthErrorMsg = "["+qp.error+"]: " +
                        (qp.error_description ? qp.error_description+ ". " : "no accessCode received from the server. ") +
                        (qp.error_uri ? "More info: "+qp.error_uri : "");
                }

                oauth2.errCb({
                    authId: oauth2.auth.name,
                    source: "auth",
                    level: "error",
                    message: oauthErrorMsg || "[Authorization failed]: no accessCode received from the server"
                });
            }
        } else {
            oauth2.callback({auth: oauth2.auth, token: qp, isValid: isValid, redirectUrl: redirectUrl});
        }
        window.close();
    }
</script>

load("@io_bazel_rules_go//go:def.bzl", "go_library")

)
